content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation PPP of length NNN. You can perform the following operation on PPP:


Choose any iii and jjj such that i<ji \lt ji<j and Pi<PjP_i \lt P_jPi​<Pj​, and remove exactly one of them from the array. After removing, the remaining parts of the array are concatenated.
Find out if it is possible to apply the given operation any number of times such that only one element remains in the array.

**Note:** A permutation of length NNN is an array where every element from 111 to NNN occurs exactly once.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains NNN - the length of the permutation PPP.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \dots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ - elements of the permutation.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if it is possible to reduce the array to a single element using the given operation. Otherwise, output `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yEs`, `yes`, `Yes` and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \dots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ form a permutation of length NNN
∑N\sum N∑N over all test cases is at most 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can apply operations as follows (the bolded elements are the pair chosen for that operation): [3,1,2,4]→[1,2,4]→[2,4]→[4][\textbf{3}, \text{1}, \text{2}, \textbf{4}] \rightarrow
[\textbf{1}, \text{2}, \textbf{4}] \rightarrow
[\textbf{2}, \textbf{4}] \rightarrow
[\text{4}][3,1,2,4]→[1,2,4]→[2,4]→[4]
Test case 222: It can be shown that it is not possible to remove all elements except one element from the array.
Test case 333: We can apply operations as follows: [5,2,3,6,1,4]→[2,3,6,1,4]→[2,3,1,4]→[3,1,4]→[1,4]→[4][\textbf{5}, \text{2}, \text{3}, \textbf{6}, \text{1}, \text{4}] \rightarrow
[\text{2}, \textbf{3}, \textbf{6}, \text{1}, \text{4}] \rightarrow
[\textbf{2}, \text{3}, \text{1}, \textbf{4}] \rightarrow
[\textbf{3}, \text{1}, \textbf{4}] \rightarrow
[\textbf{1}, \textbf{4}] \rightarrow
[\text{4}][5,2,3,6,1,4]→[2,3,6,1,4]→[2,3,1,4]→[3,1,4]→[1,4]→[4]
